subject: skills/leash_for_hooks — pre-existing functions exceed McCabe budget after D-003
principal: After D-003 flipped the audit budget from LOC to McCabe cyclomatic complexity (≤10 per function, ≤30 cumulative per module), two existing 0.1 programs in skills/leash_for_hooks fail the new audit: collectors/hook_config.py:_walk_settings (CC=12, exceeds per-function ≤10; module cumulative 35 exceeds ≤30) and resolvers/data_point.py:resolve (CC=11, exceeds per-function ≤10). The functions work correctly — they were authored under the old LOC budget and are not flagged for behavior bugs — but they exceed the new structural budget that D-003 established as the audit surface's truth.
interest: skills/leash_for_hooks/verify.py reports 2 fail / 19 self-checks until these violators are refactored or explicitly grandfathered. Any SDLC pipeline gate (CI hook, /loop verify stage, dashboard health probe) that treats verify.py exit code as a green/red signal stays red. Other skills that import the canonical audit module inherit the same surface, so similar violators may appear in claim_audit / regime_audit / leash_for_slash_commands / leash_for_symphony as their verifiers tighten.
payoff: Refactor each violator under McCabe ≤10 by extracting inner-loop / guard-chain logic into helpers. _walk_settings: split per-nesting-level (settings → hooks block → matcher list → matcher entry → inner hooks list) so each level is its own helper. resolve: split target validation, datasets-dir check, and per-jsonl scan into separate helpers. Verify by re-running radon cc on the refactored files and skills/leash_for_hooks/verify.py — both should pass without grandfathering.
